21.correct: The ancient Sanskrit text 'Natya Shastra' forms the theoretical basis of classical dance forms, including Odissi.

wrong.A: The Ramayana is an epic, not the treatise on dance.
wrong.D: The Arthashastra deals with statecraft and economics.

21.fact: The Natya Shastra, attributed to Bharata Muni, codifies dance, drama and music.

22.correct: Geologically, the Peninsular Plateau is the oldest physiographic division of India, made of ancient crystalline rocks.

wrong.A: The Himalayas are among the youngest fold mountains.
wrong.B: The Indo-Gangetic plains are recent alluvial deposits.
wrong.C: The coastal plains are relatively recent.

22.fact: The Peninsular Plateau is a part of the ancient Gondwana landmass.

23.correct: The Jagannath Temple at Puri is a classic example of the Kalinga style of temple architecture.

wrong.B: The Brihadisvara Temple is in the Dravidian style.
wrong.D: The Shore Temple (Mamallapuram) is an early Dravidian/Pallava style.

23.fact: The Kalinga style flourished in Odisha; the Konark Sun Temple is another famous example.

24.correct: A golfer may carry a maximum of 14 clubs during a round.

wrong.D: 18 exceeds the limit (18 is the number of holes, not clubs).

24.fact: Carrying more than 14 clubs incurs a penalty under the rules of golf.

25.correct: Non-ferrous minerals are characterised by the absence (lack) of iron content - e.g. copper, bauxite, lead and zinc.

wrong.C: They are not limited to construction.
wrong.D: They are not all metallic (some have non-metallic uses).

25.fact: Ferrous minerals contain iron (like iron ore, manganese); non-ferrous do not.

26.correct: The third volume of the Akbarnama is known as the 'Ain-i-Akbari', a detailed account of Akbar's administration.

wrong.B: Razmnama is the Persian translation of the Mahabharata.
wrong.C: Fatawa-i-Jahandari was written by Ziauddin Barani.

26.fact: The Akbarnama was written by Abul Fazl, Akbar's court historian.

27.correct: As of May 2025, Manan Kumar Mishra held the post of Chairman of the Bar Council of India.

wrong.B: Dushyant Dave is a senior advocate, not the BCI Chairman here.
wrong.C: Vikas Singh is a former SCBA president, not the BCI chairman cited.
wrong.D: P.S. Patwalia is a senior advocate, not the BCI chairman.

27.fact: The Bar Council of India regulates legal education and the legal profession in India.

28.correct: In November 2024, Arvinder Singh Sahney was appointed Chairman of Indian Oil Corporation (IOC).

wrong.A: K.K. Pathak is not the IOC chairman.
wrong.D: Arvind Srivastava is not the IOC chairman.

28.fact: Indian Oil Corporation is India's largest oil-marketing public-sector company.

29.correct: As of July 2025, more than about 57,897 Anganwadi centres had been upgraded to 'Saksham Anganwadi' centres.
29.fact: Saksham Anganwadis are upgraded centres with better infrastructure and nutrition support under Mission Poshan 2.0.

30.correct: The Chairperson of the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) holds office for a term of 3 years or until the age of 70, whichever is earlier.

wrong.C: The term is 3 years (after a 2019 amendment), not 6.

30.fact: The NHRC was established under the Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993.

31.correct: The Union Home Minister is the chairman of the Zonal Councils in India.

wrong.A: The President does not chair the Zonal Councils.
wrong.B: The Prime Minister does not chair them.
wrong.C: The NITI Aayog Vice-Chairman does not chair them.

31.fact: There are five Zonal Councils; states' chief ministers serve as vice-chairmen by rotation.

32.correct: Odisha is setting up the world's first black tiger safari (at Similipal, home to melanistic tigers).

wrong.A: Rajasthan is not establishing the black tiger safari.
wrong.C: Karnataka is not setting it up.
wrong.D: Andhra Pradesh is not setting it up.

32.fact: Similipal in Odisha is the only place in the world with melanistic (black) tigers.

33.correct: For NewSpace India Limited (NSIL), only statement II is correct - it handles the manufacture and in-orbit delivery of satellites; it is a public-sector (not private) enterprise, so statement I is wrong.

wrong.A: 'Only I' is wrong; NSIL is a public-sector undertaking, not private.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong because statement I is incorrect.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong because statement II is correct.

33.fact: NSIL is the commercial arm of ISRO, a Central Public Sector Enterprise.

34.correct: In December 2024, IIT Bombay developed a low-cost 'tinnitus device' with a mobile-based solution.

wrong.A: IISc Bengaluru was not credited with this device.
wrong.B: IIT Kanpur was not the developer cited.
wrong.C: JIPMER Puducherry was not the developer.

34.fact: Tinnitus is the perception of ringing or buzzing in the ears.

35.correct: The 28th National Federation Cup Senior Athletics Competition was held in 2025.
35.fact: The Federation Cup is a major national-level athletics meet in India.

36.correct: The 17th BRICS Summit was held in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.
36.fact: Brazil held the BRICS chairship in 2025, hosting the summit.

37.correct: The traditional Mizo instrument 'Tingtang' is known as the Mizo version of the guitar.

wrong.A: It is not a version of the flute.
wrong.D: It is not a pair of cymbals.

37.fact: Mizoram has a rich tradition of folk music using bamboo and string instruments.

38.correct: Pratibha Devisingh Patil took charge as the 12th President of India on 25 July 2007 (and was India's first woman President).
38.fact: Pratibha Patil served as President from 2007 to 2012.

39.correct: The Performance Grading Index (PGI) 2.0 of the Ministry of Education uses 2 main categories (domains) for evaluation.

wrong.B: 4 is not the number of main categories.

39.fact: The PGI assesses the performance of school education systems across states/UTs.

40.correct: Per the SIPRI report, North Korea was NOT among the top 5 arms importers of 2020-24 (India, Ukraine, Pakistan and others were).

wrong.A: India was among the top arms importers.
wrong.C: Ukraine was a top arms importer (2020-24).
wrong.D: Pakistan was among the top importers.

40.fact: India has long been among the world's largest arms importers, though it is boosting domestic production.
